description:
INTENT: Create a standalone JWKS client that fetches RS256 public keys from mcp-auth.snodo.dev and verifies Bearer JWTs. This is the foundational building block for OAuth 2.1 authentication on the tunnel MCP server.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Create snodo/infrastructure/jwks.py with:
  - JwksClient class with fetch() and verify(token) methods
  - JWKS from https://mcp-auth.snodo.dev/.well-known/jwks.json
  - Caches RS256 key kid=mcp-auth-rs256-v1
  - verify returns decoded payload dict or None
  - Validates RS256 signature, exp, iat
  - Errors => None + print warning to stderr
  - Uses httpx, PyJWT, RSAAlgorithm.from_jwk
  - JWKS_URI and JWKS_KID as module-level constants
  - No imports from snodo.mcp or snodo.cli
- Create tests/infrastructure/test_jwks.py with tests for:
  - Successful fetch, network/HTTP error, wrong kid
  - Valid, expired, tampered JWT, verify before fetch
  - Real RS256 keys (cryptography.hazmat), real JWTs (PyJWT)
  - Mock httpx.get for JWKS endpoint
- Do NOT modify any existing files

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. pytest tests/infrastructure/test_jwks.py -v --tb=short passes
2. ruff check snodo/infrastructure/jwks.py passes
3. from snodo.infrastructure.jwks import JwksClient, JWKS_KID, JWKS_URI works
